published_prior_art.cite: BugniGaoObradovicVelez2024: static CAR ATE/ATT identified sets, https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.08419

repo_prior_art.cite: flagship_explore/f1 is a T=3 sustained-regime full-history separation, not this two-stage pure-never-taker mixture split

published_prior_art.cite: SojitraSyrgkanis2024 and MichaelCuiLorchTchetgen2024 give dynamic point/model identification under stronger conditions, not strict nonparametric memory-bound gain; https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.01463

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_late_bounded_defiers: analogous covariate-weighted aggregation sharpness conjecture

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024, a JASA generic discrete sharp-bounds/autobounds comparator that can encode the finite latent-response LP even if it does not give the proposed closed-form splitter.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The endpoint notation and sharp sets require an explicit observational compatibility assumption that the full-memory latent-response feasible set is nonempty; §7 never states P is in the maintained model.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The 'Consequently' step from marginal sets for mu_C,0 and mu_C,1 to the sharp contrast interval needs a joint identified set or separability/product-feasibility clause; marginal intervals alone only define an outer interval.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a feasibility/nonempty-model assumption for P and define all endpoint functionals as optimizations over that nonempty latent-response polytope.

Replace the marginal interval-difference claim with a joint two-coordinate projection statement, then state the interval-difference formula only under an explicit separability result or as part of the conjectured theorem.
Add Duarte et al. 2024 as the generic sharp-bounds competitor and sharpen the novelty claim to 'closed-form analytic splitter and strict diagnostic,' not merely 'finite LP sharpness.'

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: add feasibility and joint-projection language, add the Duarte comparison, then keep the f2 kernel if the strict-gain witness remains the headline.
